```{r}
      DOY >= 355 | DOY < 80  ~ "Winter",
      DOY >= 80  & DOY < 172 ~ "Spring",
      DOY >= 172 & DOY < 266 ~ "Summer",
      DOY >= 266 & DOY < 355 ~ "Fall"
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(
    Hour >= 11, Hour <= 12,
    !is.na(F),
    Season != "Winter",
    wx.par.den15 > 800   # removes cloudy/rainy days
  ) %>%
  group_by(Site, Plot, Species, Date) %>%
  summarise(F_avg = mean(F * 3600, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")   # cm3 cm-2 s-1 -> cm3 cm-2 h-1

cat("sf_plot_avg rows:", nrow(sf_plot_avg), "\n")
print(summary(sf_plot_avg$F_avg))
```


# statistical comparison between Plots

```{r}
library(rstatix)

# =============================================================================
# sf_daily: keeps original Plot labels (UP, TR, SWAMP) -- no recoding.
# Each Site is paired-tested using whichever TWO Plot levels it actually has
# (most sites: UP vs TR; SWH: TR vs SWAMP, since it has no true UP).
# =============================================================================

sf_daily <- sf_plot_avg %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    Plot = factor(Plot, levels = c("UP", "TR", "SWAMP")),
    Site = factor(Site, levels = c("CRC", "PTR", "OWC", "GWI", "MSM", "SWH", "GCW"))
  ) %>%
  group_by(Site, Plot, Date) %>%
  summarise(F_avg = mean(F_avg, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

cat("Plot levels present per Site:\n")
sf_daily %>% distinct(Site, Plot) %>% arrange(Site, Plot) %>% print(n = Inf)

# ── Generic paired Wilcoxon test: uses whichever 2 Plot levels exist at
# each Site, instead of hardcoding UP vs TR ─────────────────────────────────

paired_test <- sf_daily %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  filter(n_distinct(Plot[!is.na(F_avg)]) == 2) %>%   # only sites with exactly 2 plots
  group_modify(~ {
    plots_here <- levels(droplevels(.x$Plot))
    wide <- .x %>%
      select(Date, Plot, F_avg) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = Plot, values_from = F_avg) %>%
      filter(!is.na(.data[[plots_here[1]]]), !is.na(.data[[plots_here[2]]]))

    x <- wide[[plots_here[1]]]
    y <- wide[[plots_here[2]]]

    w <- wilcox.test(x, y, paired = TRUE)

    tibble(
      plot_A   = plots_here[1],
      plot_B   = plots_here[2],
      n        = nrow(wide),
      median_A = round(median(x, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      median_B = round(median(y, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
      W_stat   = w$statistic,
      p_value  = w$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    ),
    contrast_label = paste(plot_A, "vs", plot_B)
  )

print(paired_test)

# =============================================================================
# Outlier trimming (Tukey IQR fence, 1.5x), per Site x Plot -- affects both
# the boxplot shape and the y-axis scale, not just visibility
# =============================================================================

sf_daily_trimmed <- sf_daily %>%
  filter(!is.na(F_avg)) %>%
  group_by(Site, Plot) %>%
  mutate(
    q1 = quantile(F_avg, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    q3 = quantile(F_avg, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    iqr = q3 - q1,
    lower_fence = q1 - 1.5 * iqr,
    upper_fence = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(F_avg >= lower_fence, F_avg <= upper_fence) %>%
  select(-q1, -q3, -iqr, -lower_fence, -upper_fence) %>%
  droplevels()   # drop unused Plot levels (e.g. UP for SWH) so facets/x-axis are clean

cat("Rows before outlier trim:", sum(!is.na(sf_daily$F_avg)), "\n")
cat("Rows after outlier trim: ", nrow(sf_daily_trimmed), "\n")

# y position per Site, computed on the TRIMMED data actually being plotted
sig_y_pos <- sf_daily_trimmed %>%
  group_by(Site) %>%
  summarise(y_pos = max(F_avg, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.05, .groups = "drop")

paired_test <- paired_test %>%
  select(-any_of("y_pos")) %>%
  left_join(sig_y_pos, by = "Site")

print(paired_test)   # confirm sig and y_pos populated, no NA

# ── Plot: x-axis shows whichever Plot levels each Site actually has ────────

ggplot(sf_daily_trimmed, aes(x = Plot, y = F_avg, fill = Plot)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_text(data = paired_test,
            aes(x = 1.5, y = y_pos, label = sig),
            vjust = 0, size = 6, fontface = "bold",
            inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~Site, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("UP" = "#A5D6AF", "TR" = "#B891C4", "SWAMP" = "#779ECB")) +
  labs(y     = "Midday Sap Flux Density (cm3 cm-2 h-1)",
       title = "Paired Wilcoxon by Site (each Site uses its own two Plot levels)",
       subtitle = "Most sites: UP vs TR; SWH: TR vs SWAMP") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
